LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BUSINESS FOR SOCIAL RESPONSIBILITY'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT OF BENJAMIN BUTLER. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEMURRER TO THE PORTION OF THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGING AGE DISCRIMINATION IS SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE A NEW FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR AGE DISCRIMINATION DISTINCT FROM HIS THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RACE DISCRIMINATION AND TO ALLEGE, IF HE IS ABLE TO DO SO, THAT SOMEONE YOUNGER THAN HIM WAS TREATED MORE FAVORABLY. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-09-2010 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-09-2010 Off Calendar

Re-Notice And Motion To Compel Further Respon Ses For Inspecdtion And Production Of Documents Sets One, And General And Employment Interrogatories
Off Calendar Nov-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Benjamin Butler, Plaintiff and moving party, in Propria Persona
Timothy L. Reed, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Business for Social Responsability

After hearing, Plaintiff Benjamin Butler's motion to compel further respones for inspection and production of documents, set one, and general and special interrogatories, is taken under submission. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition Of Pltf
Off Calendar Oct-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Eric Akra Tate, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Business for Social Responsibility
Benjamin Butler, plaintiff and opposing party in Propria Persona

Hearing held on Defendant Business for Social Responsibility's motion to compel deposition of plaintiff.
Based on Plaintiff's representation that he will appear and participate in his deposition on November 2, and 3, 2009, motion is ordered off calendar. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses For Inspection And Production Of Documents Sets One, And General And Employment Interrogatories ; Points And Authorities And Declaration In Support
Off Calendar Oct-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, Plaintiff Benjamin Butler's motion to compel further responses for instpection and production of documents, is continued to October 30, 2009 at 10:30 a.m. in Dept. 610, at the request of the moving party. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-04-2009 Set For Trial On Apr-05-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Mar-19-10 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 502

Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Answers To Interrogatories ; Statement Of Interrogaties
Off Calendar Aug-18-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Business for Social Responsibility's motion to compel production of documents and answers to interrogatories off calendar; the motion is deemed moot as a result of plaintiff's service of supplemental responses. To the extent that defendant determines plaintiff's response to be incomplete or deficient, defendant may file a motion to compel further responses. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-31-2009 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF BENJAMIN BUTLER MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Off Calendar. Motion withdrawn by moving party. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-18-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BUSINESS FOR SOCIAL RESPONSIBILITY'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT OF BENJAMIN BUTLER.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Demurrer to the portion of the third cause of action alleging age discrimination is sustained. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to allege a new fourth cause of action for age discrimination distinct from his third cause of action for race discrimination and to allege, if he is able to do so, that someone younger than him was treated more favorably. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/HEK/HEK) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077
